lines.en_lit: Of his own will, he goes to die; and he does not fulfill the will of his household, and he goes empty-handed to his household; and if only his entrance would be like his exit. And when he saw a line of people [ambuha] after him he said: “Though his excellency ascends to the heavens, and his head reaches to the clouds, yet he shall perish forever like his own dung; they who have seen him will say: Where is he?” When they would carry Rav Zutra on their shoulders during the Shabbat of the Festival he would recite “For power is not forever, and does the crown endure for all generations?”

“It is not good to respect the person of the wicked” it is not good for wicked people when they are respected in this world it not good for Ahab to be respected in this world, as it is stated: “Because he humbled himself before Me, I will not bring the evil in his days”

to turn aside the righteous in judgment” It is good for the righteous when they are not respected in this world it good for Moses that he was not respected in this world, as it is stated: “Because you did not believe in Me, to sanctify Me” Had you believed in Me, your time still would not have come to depart the world.

Fortunate are the righteous not only do they merit but they merit for their children and their children’s children until the end of all generations; as there were several sons of Aaron who deserved to be burned like Nadav and Avihu, as it is stated: who were left” But the merit of their father protected them,

Woe to the wicked, not only do they render themselves liable, but they also render their children and children’s children liable until the end of all generations. Canaan had many children deserved to be ordained like Tavi, the servant of Rabban Gamliel, but their father’s liability caused them

Whoever merit for the public will not have sin come to his hand, but whoever causes the public to sin has almost no ability to repent. What is the reason whoever merit for the public will not have sin come to his hand? so that he will not be in Gehenna while his students are in the Garden of Eden, as it is stated: “For You will not abandon my soul to the nether-world; neither will You suffer Your godly one to see the pit” whoever causes the public to sin has almost no ability to repent, he will not be in the Garden of Eden while his students are in Gehenna, as it is stated: “A man who is laden with the blood of any person shall hasten his steps to the pit; none will support him”

one who says: I will sin and I will repent, I will sin and I will repent, Why do I to say twice: I will sin and I will repent, I will sin and repent? in accordance with which Rav Huna Rav said, Rav Huna said Rav said: Once a person commits a transgression and repeats it, it becomes permitted to him. Can it enter your mind that it becomes permitted to him? Rather, it becomes to him as permitted.

I will sin and Yom Kippur atone Yom Kippur does not atone Let us say that the mishna is not in accordance with Rabbi as it was taught Rabbi says: Yom Kippur atones for all transgressions of the Torah, whether one repented or did not repent. Even you say Rabbi it is different on the basis

sins committed against God Rav Yosef bar Ḥavu raised a contradiction before Rabbi Abbahu: Yom Kippur does not atone for sins committed against a fellow person, but isn’t it written: “If one man sin against another, God [Elohim] shall judge him [ufilelo]” Who is Elohim a judge [elohim]

If so, say the latter clause “But if a man sin against the Lord, who shall entreat [yitpallel] for him?” This is is saying: If one man sins against another, God [Elohim] shall forgive him [ufilelo]; But if a man sin against the Lord, who shall entreat [yitpallel] for him? Repentance and good deeds.

Rabbi Yitzḥak said: One who angers his friend, even verbally, must appease him, as it is stated: “My son, if you have become a guarantor for your neighbor, if you have struck your hands for a stranger, you are snared by the words of your mouth… Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, seeing you have come into the hand of your neighbor. Go, humble yourself [hitrapes] and urge [rehav] your neighbor” If you have money open the palm of [hater pisat] hand to and if not, increase [harbe] friends for him,

Rav Ḥisda said: And one must appease the with three rows of three people, as it is stated: “He comes [yashor] before men, and says: I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not”

Rabbi Yosei bar Ḥanina said: Anyone who asks forgiveness of his friend should not ask more than three times, as it is stated: “Please, please forgive And now, please And if dies one brings ten people, and stands them at the grave and says I have sinned against the Lord, the God of Israel, and against so-and-so whom I wounded.

Rabbi Yirmeya Rabbi Abba, have a complaint against him. went and sat at the threshold Rabbi Abba’s When maid poured out the water the stream of water landed on head. He said They have made me into a trash heap, He recited about himself: “Who lifts up the needy out of the trash heap” Rabbi Abba heard and went out to greet him. said to him: Now I must go out to appease you as it is written: “Go, humble yourself [hitrapes] and urge your neighbor”
when Rabbi Zeira had a complaint against a person he would back and forth before him and present himself, so that could come and appease him.

Rav had a complaint against a certain butcher The butcher did not come before him On Yom Kippur eve, said: I will go appease him. He met Rav Huna, said to him: Where is my Master going? He said to him: to appease so-and-so. said: Abba is going to kill a person, went and stood by him. sitting and splitting the head raised his eyes and saw him. He said Are you Abba? Go, I have nothing to you. While he was splitting the head, one of the bones flew out and struck him in the throat and killed him,
Rav was reciting the portion before Rabbi
